title: FormatNumber<{min:0; max:1000; integer:true}> — picks uint16 (2 bytes)
description: JSON + binary (de)serialization of an ad-hoc ranged FormatNumber {min:0; max:1000; integer:true} (not one of the named int aliases); binary picks the narrowest unsigned width that fits [0,1000] — uint16 (2 bytes) — while JSON writes the plain number.
notes: Format-aware binary width is derived from arbitrary bounds, not just the named int formats: [0,1000] integer fits uint16, so each value packs into 2 bytes (getBinaryByteSizes [2,2,2]).
